commands.review_summary:
The PR skips three generated browser bundles in the database-first legacy-store scan, changes PR prepare gates from full local Vitest to broad changed-test runs, and updates focused tests.

PR surface: Tests +125, Other +8. Total +133 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main scans every `.js` under `extensions` while the bundle generators write the three browser runtime files there, and current main prepare gates still use full local `pnpm test`.

target.title: fix(ci): restore native release preparation gates
target.body:
## Summary

- Exclude only the three browser-only bundles emitted by `bundle-a2ui` and `build-diffs-viewer-runtime` from the database-first legacy-store scan.
- Refresh the six stale Codex runtime prompt snapshots so current Linux CI matches the dynamic-tool namespace protocol.
- Keep native preparation on build, static checks, and `pnpm test:changed` with its documented broad fallback; exact-SHA hosted CI remains the full isolated-suite merge gate.
- Keep the canonical `scripts/pr` runner as the only authority that can write prepare and merge artifacts.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/pr-gates.test.ts test/scripts/check-database-first-legacy-stores.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts` (479 tests)
- Testbox `tbx_01kva5x0ake08g5gt3aa3fcs38`: the same focused 479-test suite passed remotely.
- `bash -n scripts/pr-lib/gates.sh`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check scripts/pr-lib/gates.sh test/scripts/pr-gates.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Final Codex autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Hosted CI is required on the final combined head before merge.
target.author: vincentkoc
